contract validates spending intents and emits authorization events.      It does NOT move funds. The backend bridge listens for SpendAuthorized events      and executes payments from the shared Unlink spending pool.      Key properties:      - Fixed USD daily limits per EOA (set by owner / M2 signers)      - 24h rolling spend window with gas-efficient checkpoint tracking      - Nonce-based replay protection      - Transfer type validation per EOA      - No oracle dependency for spending limits (owner-settable)      Adapted from MultiSub DeFiInteractorModule spending limit pattern,      simplified for authorization-only use case.","errors":{"EnforcedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is paused."}],"ExpectedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is not paused."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"events":{"Paused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`."},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"authorizeSpend(uint256,bytes32,uint8)":{"details":"Does NOT move funds. Backend bridge listens for the event.      The caller (msg.sender) must be a registered EOA.","params":{"amount":"USD amount to authorize (18 decimals)","recipientHash":"Hashed recipient identifier (privacy)","transferType":"0=payment, 1=transfer, 2=interbank"}},"constructor":{"params":{"_avatar":"The M2 Safe address","_owner":"The owner (typically the M2 Safe itself for signers to manage)"}},"getAllowedTypesBitmap(address)":{"params":{"eoa":"The EOA address"},"returns":{"_0":"bitmap Bitmask of allowed transfer types"}},"getDailyLimit(address)":{"params":{"eoa":"The EOA EOA address"},"returns":{"_0":"remaining USD remaining in current window (18 decimals)"}},"getRollingSpend(address)":{"params":{"eoa":"The EOA address"},"returns":{"_0":"total Total USD spent in rolling 24h window (18 decimals)"}},"getSpendRecordCount(address)":{"params":{"eoa":"The EOA address"},"returns":{"_0":"count Number of active spend authorizeSpend(uint256,bytes32,uint8).details: Does NOT move funds. Backend bridge listens for the event.      The caller (msg.sender) must be a registered EOA.

params._owner: The owner (typically the M2 Safe itself for signers to manage)

returns._0: bitmap Bitmask of allowed transfer types
eoas Array of registered EOA addresses
remaining USD remaining in current window (18 decimals)
total Total USD spent in rolling 24h window (18 decimals)
count Number of active spend records
registered Whether the EOA is registered

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

params.allowedTypes: Array of allowed transfer type IDs
params.dailyLimit: Fixed USD daily spending limit (18 decimals)

params.allowedTypes: New array of allowed transfer type IDs

MAX_RECORDS_PER_EOA().notice: Maximum number of spend records to keep per EOA (gas safety)

authorizeSpend(uint256,bytes32,uint8).notice: Authorize a spending intent. Validates limits and emits event.

avatar().notice: Address of the Safe (avatar) that this module interacts with

getAllowedTypesBitmap(address).notice: Get allowed transfer types bitmap for an EOA

getDailyLimit(address).notice: Get daily limit for an EOA

getRemainingLimit(address).notice: Get remaining daily allowance for an EOA

getRollingSpend(address).notice: Get the rolling spend for an EOA within the current 24h window

getSpendRecordCount(address).notice: Get the number of spend records for an EOA

isRegisteredEOA(address).notice: Check if an EOA is registered and active

owner().notice: Owner address that can configure the module

registerEOA(address,uint256,uint8[]).notice: Register a new EOA sub-account with spending limits

registeredEOAs(uint256).notice: List of all registered EOAs (for enumeration)

target().notice: Address where transactions are sent (usually the Safe itself)

updateAllowedTypes(address,uint8[]).notice: Update allowed transfer types for an EOA

updateLimit(address,uint256).notice: Update daily spending limit for an EOA
